channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [BAJAJCON*] Bajaj Consumer Care Board Approves Q4 & FY26 Audited Financial Results
item.description:
Bajaj Consumer Care Ltd's Board of Directors met on April 17, 2026, and approved the audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and financial year ended March 31, 2026. The Auditors' Report on these results was issued with an unmodified opinion. Additionally, the Board approved the Directors’ Report, Management Discussion & Analysis Report, Corporate Governance Report, Business Responsibility & Sustainability Report, and the notice for the company's 20th Annual General Meeting.

  • Board approved audited standalone and consolidated financial results for Q4 and FY26.
  • Auditors' Report on the financial results received an unmodified opinion.
  • Other key documents like Directors' Report and AGM notice were also approved.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Bajaj Consumer Care Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [BONLON] Bonlon Industries clarifies on recent price movement
item.description:
Bonlon Industries Ltd has submitted a clarification regarding its recent share price movement, responding to a notice from BSE dated April 16, 2026. The company informed that it has consistently and timely intimated all material events and information as required under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, to both BSE Limited and National Stock Exchange of India Limited. Bonlon Industries confirmed that no information has been withheld and reiterated its regular compliance with all relevant disclosure requirements to the stock exchanges.

  • Bonlon Industries responded to BSE query on price movement.
  • Company asserts compliance with SEBI disclosure regulations.
  • Confirmed no material information has been withheld.
  • Aims to address market concerns regarding stock volatility.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Bonlon Industries Ltd | Category: Clarification

item.title: [ODYCORP] Odyssey Board approves new MOA/AOA, RPTs, loans; postal ballot set
item.description:
Odyssey Corporation Ltd's Board of Directors met on April 17, 2026, and approved several key resolutions, all subject to shareholder approval. These include the adoption of new Memorandum of Association (MOA) and Articles of Association (AOA) in line with the Companies Act, 2013. The Board also approved material related party transactions with V-Vanguard Developers Private Limited and the ability to advance loans, give guarantees, or provide security under Section 185 of the Companies Act, 2013. To obtain shareholder consent for these items, a postal ballot notice was approved, along with a calendar of events and the appointment of M/s Jaymin Modi & Co. as Scrutinizer for the process.

  • Board approved new MOA and AOA, subject to shareholder vote.
  • Material related party transactions with V-Vanguard Developers approved.
  • Approval for loans/guarantees under Companies Act, 2013 granted.
  • Shareholder approval to be sought via postal ballot and e-voting.
  • Scrutinizer appointed for the postal ballot process.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: ODYSSEY CORPORATION LTD. | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [SAFFRON] Saffron Industries clarifies no undisclosed material info after price surge
item.description:
Saffron Industries Ltd. responded to an exchange query regarding significant movement in its share price. The company clarified that there is no material information, event, or impending announcement, as per Regulation 30 of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, that has not been disclosed and could impact the share price. They stated that all material information has been promptly disclosed. The company believes the recent price movement is purely market-driven, influenced by general market conditions and investor sentiment, particularly in the real estate sector. Saffron Industries, engaged in real estate development, reiterated that there are no undisclosed material developments related to its projects, operations, or financial performance that could have caused the observed price movement.

  • Saffron Industries clarified no undisclosed material information.
  • Response to exchange query on significant share price movement.
  • Company attributes price change to market forces and sentiment.
  • No undisclosed developments in real estate projects or operations.
  • Reiterates compliance with SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Saffron Industries Limited | Category: Clarification

item.title: [KESORAMIND] Kesoram Industries' Income Tax Demand Reduced to ₹6.03 Cr
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Kesoram Industries Ltd. announced an update on an income tax demand. The company had previously received an assessment order for AY 2024-25, raising a demand of ₹70.54 Crores. Following the company's representation, the demand has now been significantly reduced to ₹6.03 Crores. Kesoram Industries is currently reviewing the revised order and evaluating potential further legal remedies for the remaining balance. The company stated that this matter has no material adverse impact on its operations.

  • Initial income tax demand was ₹70.54 Cr for AY 2024-25.
  • Demand significantly reduced to ₹6.03 Cr after company's representation.
  • Company evaluating further legal remedies for the remaining demand.
  • No material adverse impact on company operations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: KESORAM INDUSTRIES LTD. | Category: Tax & Penalty

item.title: [SAUMYA] Saumya Consultants Appoints ALPS & CO. as Internal Auditor for FY2026-27
item.description:
Saumya Consultants Ltd. announced that its Board of Directors, in a meeting held on April 17, 2026, approved the appointment of M/s ALPS & CO., Chartered Accountants, as the Internal Auditor for the Financial Year 2026-27. M/s ALPS & CO., a peer-reviewed partnership firm managed by CA. Ashok Kumar Khetawat, specializes in risk-based internal audits, due diligence, and assessment of internal finance controls. The appointment is effective from the date of the board meeting.

  • Board approved the appointment of an internal auditor.
  • M/s ALPS & CO. appointed as Internal Auditor for FY 2026-27.
  • Appointment was made effective from April 17, 2026.
  • ALPS & CO. is a peer-reviewed firm specializing in risk-based audits.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Saumya Consultants Ltd | Category: Auditor

item.title: [GENUSPRIME] Genus Prime Infra: 3.22 Cr shares allotted via Scheme of Arrangement
item.description:
Genus Prime Infra Ltd received a disclosure under SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares & Takeovers) Regulations, 2011, from Simple Agarwal and other acquirers. This relates to the allotment of 3,22,35,205 equity shares to them. The allotment occurred pursuant to a Scheme of Arrangement involving Genus Prime Infra Limited, Sansar Infrastructure Private Limited, Star Vanijya Private Limited, Sunima Trading Private Limited, Genus Power Infrastructures Limited, and Yajur Commodities Limited, which was approved by NCLT on April 24, 2025. The acquirers are exempted from making an open offer under Regulation 10(1)(d)(ii) of the SEBI Takeover Regulations. The filing includes detailed pre and post-transaction shareholding of various entities.

  • 3,22,35,205 equity shares of Genus Prime Infra allotted to various acquirers.
  • Allotment resulted from an NCLT-approved Scheme of Arrangement dated April 24, 2025.
  • Acquirers are exempt from making an open offer under SEBI Regulation 10(1)(d)(ii).
  • Disclosure provides detailed pre and post-transaction shareholding for multiple entities.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Genus Prime Infra Ltd | Category: Shareholding

item.title: [CRISIL] CRISIL Q1 FY26: Income up 30.1%, PBT up 35.7%, PAT up 45.9%; Dividend ₹9
item.description:
🔥 Trending

CRISIL Ltd reported strong unaudited financial results for Q1 FY26. Consolidated income from operations rose 30.1% to ₹1,057.7 crore, while total income increased 29.6% to ₹1,093.7 crore. Profit before tax (PBT) grew 35.7% to ₹308.4 crore, and profit after tax (PAT) surged 45.9% to ₹233.3 crore. The company declared an interim dividend of ₹9 per share. A favorable foreign exchange movement contributed a gain of ₹14.4 crore to PBT. The Managing Director & CEO highlighted growth driven by customer centricity and domain-led solutions, with a focus on market share expansion and investment in GenAI. Crisil Ratings revenue grew 18.9%, with the overall ratings segment revenue up 20.2%.

  • Q1 FY26 income from operations up 30.1% to ₹1,057.7 crore.
  • Profit before tax (PBT) increased 35.7% to ₹308.4 crore.
  • Profit after tax (PAT) surged 45.9% to ₹233.3 crore.
  • Board declared an interim dividend of ₹9 per share.
  • Ratings segment revenue grew 20.2% year-on-year.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: CRISIL LTD. | Category: Quarterly Updates

item.title: [HRMNYCP] Rajesh Ghosh acquires 30.10% stake in Harmony Capital
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Rajesh Ghosh has acquired 36,50,000 equity shares, representing 30.10% of Harmony Capital Services Limited's total share capital. The acquisition, made through a preferential allotment and open offer, took place on April 15, 2026. Post-acquisition, the company's total equity share capital increased from 30,00,900 to 1,21,26,900 shares. Rajesh Ghosh is identified as belonging to the Promoter/Promoter group, and this disclosure was made under Regulation 29(1) of SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares & Takeovers) Regulations, 2011.

  • Rajesh Ghosh acquired 30.10% of Harmony Capital Services.
  • The acquisition involved 36,50,000 equity shares.
  • Shares were acquired via preferential allotment and open offer.
  • The acquirer is part of the Promoter/Promoter group.
  • Total equity capital increased significantly post-acquisition.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: HARMONY CAPITAL SERVICES LTD. | Category: Shareholding

item.title: [CROMPTON] Crompton Greaves secures ₹71.75 Cr solar pumping system order
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Crompton Greaves Consumer Electricals Ltd has received an order from Maharashtra State Electricity Distribution Company Limited (MSEDCL) for approximately ₹71.75 Crores (excluding GST). The contract involves the design, manufacture, supply, installation, testing, and commissioning of 3540 Off-grid DC Solar Photovoltaic Water Pumping Systems (SPWPS) under the MTSKPY/PM-KUSUM-B scheme across various locations in Maharashtra. The order is to be executed within 60 days from the date of issue of the Notice to Proceed.

  • Crompton Greaves secured a ₹71.75 Cr order from MSEDCL.
  • The contract is for 3540 solar photovoltaic water pumping systems.
  • Work includes design, manufacture, supply, installation, testing, and commissioning.
  • The project is under the MTSKPY/PM-KUSUM-B scheme in Maharashtra.
  • Execution is required within 60 days from the work order date.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Crompton Greaves Consumer Electricals Ltd | Category: Award
